description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crab, featuring a broad, rounded carapace covered in a rough, textured surface with small spines and setae. The crab's coloration is predominantly a muted, sandy brown with subtle variations and lighter patches on the underside and joints. Its two prominent claws (chelae) are robust and slightly asymmetrical, with visible articulation at the joints. The crab has multiple walking legs, each jointed and covered in fine bristles, splaying outward from the body. The eyes are small, dark, and positioned toward the front of the carapace, giving the crab a forward-facing gaze. No antennae are clearly visible, and the overall appearance suggests a species adapted for camouflage in sandy or rocky environments.
description: A sandy brown crab with a rough, spiny carapace, robust claws, and multiple jointed legs, featuring small forward-facing eyes.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's broad carapace, both claws, and forward-facing eyes.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the crab's body depth, leg articulation, and one claw in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the curvature of the carapace and the tucked position of the legs.
